# sdlc-mcp

A foundational SDLC repository that serves as the persistent memory layer and AI development toolkit across all agentic tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Roo/Cline, Windsurf, and others).

## Purpose

This repo gives AI assistants:
- Structured knowledge of the full software development lifecycle
- Active slash commands for creating SDLC deliverables
- A live state layer so any session in any tool can pick up where the last one left off

## How to orient at session start

Read these files in order:

1. `sdlc/overview.md` — SDLC phases, rules, and how this repo works
2. `docs/memory/project-state.md` — current phase and open work
3. `AGENTS.md` — core agent rules, conventions, and tools

Then tell the user what you found and ask: "We are in the [phase] phase. Open work: [list]. Last session: [summary]. Where would you like to pick up?"

## Available tools

- **context7** (stdio MCP): fetches current npm/framework docs on demand
- **sdlc-gitmcp** (this server, SSE): live read access to this repo's files and state
- **sdlc** (stdio MCP, local): exposes slash commands

## Available commands (local)

- `/generate_prd` — scaffold a Product Requirements Document
- `/create_technical_design` — run full design phase (technical design + ADRs + checklist)
- `/create_adr` — create a single Architecture Decision Record
- `/plan_sprint` — create a sprint plan
- `/gen_test_plan` — test plan scaffold
- `/release_notes` — draft from git log

## Subagent System

This repo uses a structured subagent architecture coordinated by the orchestrator.

Main agents:
- Research Agent
- Design Agent
- Implementation Agent
- Review Agent

Full rules and spawn triggers: `sdlc/agents/orchestrator.md`

All subagent outputs are written to `docs/agents/<date>-<role>-<slug>.md` and committed immediately.

## Key files

- `sdlc/overview.md` — overall SDLC phases and rules
- `AGENTS.md` — core agent rules and conventions
- `sdlc/agents/orchestrator.md` — orchestrator and subagent triggers
- `sdlc/planning/README.md` — planning phase workflow and templates
- `sdlc/design/README.md` — design phase workflow and templates
- `docs/memory/project-state.md` — live project state (always read at session start)

## Conventions

- Language: TypeScript, ESM, Node 18+
- Commits: Conventional Commits (`feat:`, `fix:`, `chore:`, `docs:`, `test:`, `refactor:`)
- Branch naming: `feat/`, `fix/`, `chore/`, `docs/`
- Never commit directly to `main`
- Never edit generated `config/*.json` files directly — edit `config/config-sources.json` then run `npm run sync:configs`

## Session closing protocol

Before ending a session, commit all new or updated documents:

git add docs/
git commit -m "docs([phase]): [brief description]"
git push

This ensures the next session — in any tool — can read current state via GitMCP.
